data.title: Is the stock market open on Black Friday? Are any stores open on Thanksgiving Day?
data.description:
By Charles Passy

Thanksgiving week comes with plenty of holiday-adjusted business hours, including, of course on Wall Street - so here's what you need to know

A scene from a past Macy's Thanksgiving Day Parade - which will be up and running on Turkey Day morning, unlike the stock market and many stores and services.

Happy Turkey Day!

Yes, Thanksgiving is Thursday. The holiday has been recognized by the federal government since 1870, though President Abraham Lincoln earlier had encouraged Americans to recognize a November "day of Thanksgiving" in 1863, and President George Washington had issued a proclamation for "a day of public thanksgiving and prayer" on Nov. 26, 1789.

Now, of course, there's another "holiday" on the day after Thanksgiving -namely, the shopping extravaganza known as Black Friday.

So, what will be open (or closed) on Thanksgiving and Black Friday? Here's what to expect.

Stock and bond markets

U.S. stock and bond markets will be closed on Thanksgiving Day, and they will have shortened hours on Black Friday. The New York Stock Exchange and the Nasdaq exchange will call time on equity-market trading SPX COMP DJIA at 1 p.m. Eastern on the Friday after Thanksgiving, while bond markets BX:TMUBMUSD10Y will remain open until 2 p.m. on Black Friday.

Mail and packages

The U.S. Postal Service will not be delivering mail on Thanksgiving, but service will resume on Black Friday. FedEx (FDX) will not offer service on Thanksgiving, either, except for "Custom Critical" deliveries, and FedEx will have modified service on Black Friday. UPS (UPS) will not deliver on Thanksgiving, except for "Express Critical" items, but the company says delivery and pickup services will be available again on Black Friday.

Banks

Banks are generally closed on Thanksgiving (though you can always visit an ATM or use your banking app). But they will be open on Black Friday, according to GoBankingRates, though some branches could limit their hours.

Government offices

Since Thanksgiving is indeed a federal holiday, all nonessential federal government offices are closed (and the same generally applies to state and municipal government offices).

Black Friday is not a federal holiday. But it is a state holiday in more than 20 states, according to the OfficeHolidays website, so check your local listings.

Schools

Schools are closed on Thanksgiving, and most are closed on Black Friday, as well, but it's best to check with your local district to be sure.

Stores

While many stores had started opening on Thanksgiving years ago as a way to pull Black Friday sales forward, that trend appears to be reversing. Most major retailers, among them Costco (COST), Walmart (WMT) and Target (TGT) are closed this Thanksgiving. The exceptions: Some grocery chains, dollar stores and pharmacies are open, but with adjusted hours (think stores like CVS (CVS) and Whole Foods (AMZN)). Black Friday, of course, is a big shopping day, with some stores opening as early as 5 a.m. Check with individual stores to find exact hours.

-Charles Passy

This content was created by MarketWatch, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. MarketWatch is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: Wipro signs multi-year deal with Odido Netherlands to transform IT landscape
data.description:
IT services major Wipro Ltd on Thursday (November 27) announced a multi-year engagement with Odido Netherlands B.V. to transform its IT landscape and enhance customer experience across both enterprise and consumer segments.

By combining AI and deep consulting expertise, Wipro will help Odido improve customer engagement and satisfaction, improve productivity, and streamline operations to reduce costs.

A key feature of the engagement is the use of a self-funded model in which productivity-driven savings are reinvested to continuously fund new digital initiatives, ensuring that innovation remains sustainable and scalable.

Under the agreement, Wipro will lead a full-scale modernisation of Odido's digital and enterprise technology landscape and drive IT simplification and automation. The transformation will be powered by Wipro’s WEGA and WINGS AI delivery platforms, part of Wipro Intelligence, the company’s unified suite of AI-powered platforms, solutions, and transformative offerings.

These capabilities will enable Odido to build and manage a dynamic ecosystem of agent-driven services and solutions designed to improve service reliability, IT operations, and incident resolution rates.

Wipro’s conversational AI chat solution will further support Odido by enhancing responsiveness and resilience across its technology ecosystem through contextualised, multilingual, and conversational self-service capabilities.

Wipro’s experience-led studio model, powered by Designit, will be a central part of the engagement. By integrating Wipro’s design studio with its development, design, and engineering teams, user and business needs will be captured early, seamlessly translated into solutions, and delivered with speed and excellence.

This collaboration will help Odido accelerate time to market and consistently deliver high-quality digital experiences.

"At Odido, our goal is to make technology more human. Everything we do is driven by a humancentric approach. This applies not only to our network services and product innovations, but also to our processes, operations, and IT infrastructure,” said Robert Purdy, Chief IT Officer at Odido Netherlands.

Graziella Neuvéglise, Managing Director, Western Europe, Wipro, said, “Odido's trust in Wipro reflects our ability to deliver strategic value through intelligent operations, agile development, and a future-ready IT strategy. This transformation is consulting-led, with our teams shaping the roadmap, optimising processes, and aligning technology with business goals."

Shares of Wipro Ltd ended at ₹249.40, up by ₹0.75, or 0.30%, on the BSE.

data.title: Sweden's Debt Office Expects Widening Budget Deficit, Increases Borrowing Plans — 2nd Update
data.description:
By Emese Bartha

Sweden's central government will need to increase its borrowing due to a growing budget deficit expected for both 2026 and 2027, mainly on the back of an expansionary fiscal policy, the National Debt Office said.

In a borrowing review on Thursday, the NDO said it expects a budget deficit of 103 billion kronor ($10.85 billion) for 2025, up from 93 billion kronor in the previous forecast in May.

It expects a deficit of 173 billion kronor next year, up from the 89 billion-kronor forecast in May. The deficit is expected to rise to 194 billion kronor in 2027, which is included in the NDO's forecasts for the first time.

"This is mainly due to expansionary fiscal policy," the NDO said.

For 2025 and 2026, the deficit--or net borrowing requirement--is now a total of 94 billion kronor larger than in the previous forecast in May, the NDO said.

In morning trading, Swedish government bonds were underperforming their European peers, suggesting that the volume of bond-issuance increase might have caught markets off-guard. The 10-year Swedish bond yield was up 3.8 basis points to 2.702%, while the 10-year German Bund yield was up 0.8 basis point at 2.683%, according to Tradeweb.

The NDO will finance the widening budget deficit primarily by increasing the issuance of government bonds and treasury bills. The increase will affect nominal government bonds, as the NDO has left the issuance plans for inflation-linked bonds and foreign currency bonds unchanged.

"All in all, the borrowing requirement increased more than expected," Nordea analyst Joel Lundh said in a note. "Fiscal reforms and higher expenditures towards defence and military aid to Ukraine weigh on the budget during the forecast horizon," he said.

Swedbank analysts say they are skeptical that the borrowing requirement in 2026 will be as large as the NDO forecasts. "There is a risk that capital-market borrowing will be revised down in the next borrowing report," they said.

The NDO increased its forecast for nominal bond issuance to 216 billion kronor for 2026 from 146 billion kronor, and it expects to issue 220 billion kronor in these assets in 2027.

"In order to meet the growing borrowing requirement, the debt office is first increasing the issuance volume of nominal government bonds from 6 billion kronor to 8 billion kronor per auction from the beginning of 2026," it said.

The debt office will also consider holding more frequent auctions for nominal government bonds after the next central government borrowing report, which will be published on May 28, it said.

The NDO now plans to issue 6 billion kronor in linkers in 2026 and also in 2027, and it penciled in the issuance of 19 billion kronor in foreign currency bonds in 2026 and 2027.

The NDO confirmed its previous plans to introduce two new nominal government bonds in 2026, both in the 10-year maturity segment.

The debt office will carry out the majority of the auctions in the 10-year segment, as well as in the two- and five-year reference bonds, it said.

"This is in line with the strategy of borrowing in a transparent and predictable manner with a focus on building up nominal government bonds as reference bonds," the NDO said.

Write to Emese Bartha at emese.bartha@wsj.com

data.title: Croatia’s GDP growth slows to 2.3% in the third quarter
data.description:
Croatia’s economy grew 2.3% year-on-year in the third quarter of 2025, slowing from an upwardly revised expansion of 3.6% in the previous quarter, as weaker exports and softer household consumption offset investment growth, preliminary data from the country's statistics office showed on Thursday.

On a seasonally adjusted basis, gross domestic product (GDP) rose 0.3% compared with the previous three months.

Household consumption rose 1.9% year-on-year, down sharply from 4.0% in the previous quarter, the stats office said.

Overall final consumption increased 2.3%, while government spending expanded 3.8%, partially offsetting the weaker household demand.

Exports of goods and services fell 1.1% compared with a year earlier, dragged down by a 4.6% drop in services, while goods exports managed a 4.1% increase. Imports rose 2.4%, driven by a 12.7% surge in services.

Gross fixed capital formation jumped 7.5%, making investments the strongest contributor to GDP, following a 4.9% rise in the second quarter.

data.title: Here’s why Ashok Leyland shares jumped over 7% on Thursday
data.description:
Shares of Ashok Leyland Ltd gained 6.67% on Thursday to close at ₹158.88 on the NSE after touching an intraday high of ₹162, driven by a mix of positive demand indicators and a restructuring move within the Hinduja Group.

Market participants pointed to strong November VAHAN retail registrations ahead of the company’s monthly sales announcement on Monday, December 1. Retail numbers for November 1–27, have already surpassed the full-month tally for November 2024, coming in at 13,027 units versus 12,923 units.

Sentiment was further boosted by the NDL Ventures board approval to merge Hinduja Leyland Finance (HLF) into NDL Ventures — a move that analysts say could unlock value for Ashok Leyland, which owns a 61% stake in HLF.

The reverse-merger will also enable the group’s NBFC arm to get listed. As per the scheme, HLF shareholders will receive 25 NDL Ventures shares for every 10 HLF shares, with the record date to be announced later.

Analysts noted that HLF’s true valuation was not fully reflected in Ashok Leyland’s stock price, and that the merger could help close that gap.

The stock has also been supported by improving fundamentals. Earlier this month, Ashok Leyland crossed a long-standing market-share barrier in the medium and heavy commercial vehicle (MHCV) category. Managing Director and CEO Shenu Agarwal said the company had moved past the 29.5–30% range to reach 31% in the first half of the year. “We have achieved 31% and therefore now we should try and touch 32% and beyond,” he said.

The company is also stepping up its export strategy, with a target of 25,000 units over the next two to three years. Meanwhile, the broader CV cycle has strengthened following the recent GST rate cut, with October’s festive and seasonal demand lifting Ashok Leyland’s sales by 16%, outperforming industry estimates of around 11%. A notable rebound in the bus segment, where sales rose 34%, has also contributed to improved sentiment.

data.title: Stalprodukt Unit Signs Zinc-Lead Ore Supply Contract With Ocean Partners UK Limited
data.description:
Stalprodukt SA :

  • UNIT SIGNS ZINC-LEAD ORE SUPPLY CONTRACT WITH OCEAN PARTNERS UK LIMITED

  • UNDER CONTRACT COMPANY TO RECEIVE IN TOTAL 1.5 MILLION WMT OF ORE

  • ANNUAL VALUE OF PURCHASED ORE ESTIMATED AT AROUND $55 MILLION BASED ON CURRENT PRICES

  • PURCHASE OF ORE IN SPECIFIED QUANTITY WILL ENABLE PRODUCTION OF ABOUT 43,000 TONNES OF SULPHIDE CONCENTRATES PER YEAR FOR ZINC PRODUCTION AT EXISTING PLANT

  • CONCLUSION OF DEAL, IF EFFECTIVELY IMPLEMENTED, WILL SECURE SUPPLY OF ZINC CONCENTRATE IN AMOUNT SUFFICIENT TO MEET ABOUT 45% OF COMPANY'S DEMAND FOR SULPHIDE CONCENTRATE

  • UNIT ZGH BOLESLAW TO INCUR 37 MILLION ZLOTY IN CAPITAL EXPENDITURE RELATED TO CONTRACT IMPLEMENTATION

data.title: ADJ: Asset disposals, stable Berlin growth, and strong liquidity support 2025 guidance
data.description:
Major asset disposals and strong Berlin rental growth offset lower net rental income from portfolio reductions. LTV rose to 73.5%, but liquidity and debt maturities are well managed, with 97% of debt due 2028 or later. Net rental income guidance for 2025 is confirmed.
